title: potentilla subacaulis var. velutina
intro: Potentilla subacaulis var. velutina, also known as velvety cinquefoil, is a perennial herbaceous plant belonging to the Rosaceae family. It is used in traditional medicine for its astringent, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections, due to its ability to reduce inflammation and promote healing. Velvety cinquefoil can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture for medicinal purposes, and its roots and leaves can be used fresh or dried for herbal remedies.
